title: Secure password storage practices
description: Storing passwords in plaintext or using fast hashing algorithms makes them vulnerable to theft and cracking. Proper password storage requires strong, slow hashing algorithms specifically designed for password security.
recommendation: Use slow, cryptographically secure password hashing algorithms like bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

noncompliant.description: Using plaintext storage or fast hashing algorithms (MD5, SHA1, SHA256) makes passwords vulnerable to theft and brute-force attacks.

compliant.description: Using slow, cryptographically secure hashing algorithms designed specifically for password storage like bcrypt or scrypt.

checks.message: Use secure password storage practices - avoid hardcoding and weak hashing
